theory.canonical_statement: ID4648 records Unresolved Source Fragment (Pending Review) as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `0_Incoming\Manual_Uploads\TXT_20260224_182616\yes thats  great lets please do, -153a4e1ae6baa2aa.txt` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: $$ DECLARE input_x FLOAT8; input_y FLOAT8; input_z FLOAT8; lat FLOAT8; lon FLOAT8; best_index INTEGER; best_dot FLOAT8 := -2.0; current_dot FLOAT8; result daansphere_point; BEGIN -- Extract lat/lon from PostGIS geography lat := ST_Y(point_geog::geometry); lon := ST_X(point_geog::geometry); -- Convert to Cartesian (this is what PostGIS does internally anyway) input_x := cos(radians(lat)) * cos(radians(lon)); input_y := cos(radians(lat)) * sin(radians(lon)); input_z := sin(radians(lat)); -- Find nearest DAANSphere point using dot product -- This is the key optimization - no trigonometry needed! SELECT sphere_index, (x * input_x + y * input_y + z * input_z) as dot_product INTO best_index, best_ ...
canonical_equation.source_section_latex: $$ DECLARE input_x FLOAT8; input_y FLOAT8; input_z FLOAT8; lat FLOAT8; lon FLOAT8; best_index INTEGER; best_dot FLOAT8 := -2.0; current_dot FLOAT8; result daansphere_point; BEGIN -- Extract lat/lon from PostGIS geography lat := ST_Y(point_geog::geometry); lon := ST_X(point_geog::geometry); -- Convert to Cartesian (this is what PostGIS does internally anyway) input_x := cos(radians(lat)) * cos(radians(lon)); input_y := cos(radians(lat)) * sin(radians(lon)); input_z := sin(radians(lat)); -- Find nearest DAANSphere point using dot product -- This is the key optimization - no trigonometry needed! SELECT sphere_index, (x * input_x + y * input_y + z * input_z) as dot_product INTO best_index, best_ ...

unrealized_section_cleanup.reason: no generated Wolfram, derivation, or proof artifact is included until real source-backed content exists

proof_pipeline.status: source-truth provenance linked to generated proof-obligation and certificate artifacts
proof_pipeline.scope_note: This metadata records authorship, lineage, and proof-pipeline artifact links. It does not by itself assert that the entry has a completed native analytic proof.

verification_summary.wolfram_spine_checks: passed for generated curated/Zenodo spine checks; energy-matter naive integral divergence warning is expected

wolfram_form.existing_from_tex: $$ DECLARE input_x FLOAT8; input_y FLOAT8; input_z FLOAT8; lat FLOAT8; lon FLOAT8; best_index INTEGER; best_dot FLOAT8 := -2.0; current_dot FLOAT8; result daansphere_point; BEGIN -- Extract lat/lon from PostGIS geography lat := ST_Y(point_geog::geometry); lon := ST_X(point_geog::geometry); -- Convert to Cartesian (this is what PostGIS does internally anyway) input_x := cos(radians(lat)) * cos(radians(lon)); input_y := cos(radians(lat)) * sin(radians(lon)); input_z := sin(radians(lat)); -- Find nearest DAANSphere point using dot product -- This is the key optimization - no trigonometry needed! SELECT sphere_index, (x * input_x + y * input_y + z * input_z) as dot_product INTO best_index, best_ ...
